data.title: Starbucks' $100M Nashville investment fuels speculation over Seattle exit
data.description:
As part of the company’s efforts to expand coffeehouses across the Southeast, Starbucks (SBUX) will invest $100M to open a corporate office in Nashville, Tennessee, to employ as many as 2K workers over the next five years. Besides state tax breaks, Starbucks (SBUX) will receive a year’s free rent at the new location.

Certain operations currently being conducted at the Seattle headquarters will be moved to Nashville once the facility is completed in 2027. These include roles tied to the Southeast expansion, functions previously outsourced to contractors, and certain unspecified roles currently performed by Seattle-based employees. Some technology positions in Seattle will also be transferred to Nashville, most likely to avoid a 6.5% Washington state tax on IT services.

While most of the company’s corporate staff will remain in Seattle, Washington’s tax structure and business climate have led to speculation that Niccol’s move to Nashville may signal a gradual shift away from Seattle. Last November, Seattle’s then mayor-elect Katie Wilson urged a boycott of the company during a strike of the company’s baristas.

While at Chipotle (CMG), Niccol moved the company’s headquarters to California from Denver, Colorado.

Separately, beginning May 11, Starbucks (SBUX) customers can order coffee up to one hour ahead of time using the Mobile Order & Pay app.

Scheduled ordering allows customers to select a pickup time when placing their order instead of ordering for immediate pickup.

Powered by Starbucks’ (SBUX) Smart Queue algorithm, pickup times reflect real-time availability at each location.

data.title: NVR stock slides after Q1 earnings disappoint
data.description:
NVR (NVR) stock dropped 5.9% in Wednesday midday trading after the homebuilder and mortgage banking company's Q1 earnings failed to meet the Wall Street consensus amid a weak housing market.

Q1 EPS of $67.76,  missing the average analyst estimate of $79.20, declined from $94.83 in the year-ago period.

Q1 revenue of $1.88B, vs. the $2.02B consensus, decreased from $2.41B a year ago.

Settlements in Q1 sank 22% Y/Y to 4,015, primarily from a 15% lower backlog balance of homes sold but not settled entering Q1 2026 compared to the same period in 2025. The average settlement price of $457K stayed flat from the year-ago quarter.

New orders rose 7% to 5,738 units, while the average sales price of new orders slipped 2% to $440.1K.

The cancellation rate improved to 14% from 16% in Q1 2025.

Homebuilding revenue of $1.83B fell 22%. Gross profit margin narrowed to 19.6% from 21.9% a year ago on pricing pressure and higher lot costs. 

Mortgage banking loan production totaled $1.05B, down 27% Y/Y. 

NVR's (NVR) effective tax rate in Q1 declined to 21.2% from 25.5% in the same period last year.

data.title: Roche to seek regulatory review for multiple sclerosis drug despite fatalities
data.description:
Roche (RHHBF) (RHHBY) has announced plans to seek regulatory review for an experimental therapy for multiple sclerosis, an autoimmune condition affecting the central nervous system, even as its late-stage trial data indicated several patient deaths.

On Tuesday, the Swiss drugmaker shared new data from its Phase 3 FENhance 1 and 2 studies, which tested its MS therapy fenebrutinib against teriflunomide, a rival drug marketed by Sanofi (SNY) as Aubagio.

The oral Bruton’s tyrosine kinase ((BTK)) inhibitor had already met the primary endpoints related to annualized relapse rates in FENhance 1 and 2, as well as one related to non-inferiority against Roche’s (RHHBY) own MS therapy Ocrevus in its FENtrepid Phase 3 trial.

However, citing seven deaths in the fenebrutinib arm across both FENhance 1 and 2 trials, the company attributed the fatalities to factors including infections and complications linked to type 1 diabetes.

“The collective positive results across all three pivotal studies demonstrate that fenebrutinib consistently showed a profound benefit on relapsing and progressive disease biology,” Roche (RHHBY) said, adding that it will submit data from all three trials to regulators.

In February, citing epidemiological studies, the company said that fatality rates are higher in MS patients compared to the general population after results from FENtrepid indicated a 1.4% mortality rate in the fenebrutinib arm.

data.title: Masco maintains 2026 EPS $4.10-$4.30 while lifting 2026 deployment to at least $800M
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Masco Corporation (MAS) Q1 2026

Management view

  • "Jai Shah, Group President, Plumbing and Wellness; and Rick Marshall, Vice President of Masco Operating System, recently announced their intent to retire from Masco later this summer" (President, CEO & Director Jonathon Nudi). "With Jai's retirement... the leaders of our 4 largest businesses: Delta, Hansgrohe, BEHR and Watkins Wellness will now all report directly to me... we are adding 2 new leaders to our executive committee with expertise in supply chain and procurement."
  • "Our integration of Liberty Hardware into Delta Faucet Company is on track" and "Restructuring actions to streamline our business, reduce headcount and optimize operations are ongoing" (President, CEO & Director Jonathon Nudi). "We incurred approximately $8 million in restructuring charges in the first quarter, and we continue to expect approximately $50 million in total charges in 2026."
  • "Net sales increased 6%" and "Earnings per share grew 20% during the quarter to $1.04 per share" (President, CEO & Director Jonathon Nudi). "Plumbing product sales increased 7% in local currency" with "International plumbing sales increased 1% in local currency... partially offset by the ongoing weak market in China."
  • "Our strong cash flow allowed us to return $267 million to shareholders this quarter" (President, CEO & Director Jonathon Nudi). "We believe it is prudent to maintain our 2026 earnings per share guidance in the range of $4.10 to $4.30 per share" and "our sales will now be up low single digits for 2026" (President, CEO & Director Nudi).
  • "Operating profit grew 13% to $324 million" and "Gross margin expanded 10 basis points to 36%" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Richard Westenberg). "Operating profit was driven by pricing actions and cost savings initiatives partially offset by higher tariff and commodity costs."

Outlook

  • "We expect 2026 sales to be up low single digits versus our previous guide of flat to up low single digits" and "we continue to expect our margins to expand to approximately 17%" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Richard Westenberg). "We anticipate total Masco margin to be relatively flat in the first half of the year versus our previous guide of margin contraction and to expand in the second half of the year."
  • "We are maintaining our 2026 EPS estimate of $4.10 to $4.30 per share" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg). "This now assumes a 200 million average diluted share count for the year versus our previous guide of 202 million shares and a 24.5% effective tax rate."
  • "In our Plumbing segment, we continue to expect 2026 full year sales to be up low single digits and our operating margin to expand to approximately 18%" and "In our Decorative Architectural segment, we continue to expect 2026 sales to be roughly flat... and our operating margin to be approximately 19%" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg).

Financial results

  • "Total sales increasing 6%" and "SG&A as a percent of sales was 19.1%, 80 basis points lower than the prior year" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Richard Westenberg). "Operating profit grew 13% to $324 million" and "our margin expanded 90 basis points to 16.9%."
  • "Plumbing sales increased 9% in the first quarter or 7%, excluding the favorable impact of currency" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg). "Pricing actions... increased sales by 6%" and "volume... was up slightly."
  • "Decorative Architectural sales were in line with the prior year" and "Operating profit in the first quarter was $105 million" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg). "We continue to anticipate full year PRO paint sales to increase mid-single digits and for DIY paint sales to decrease mid-single digits."
  • "We finished the quarter with $1.3 billion of liquidity" and "gross debt-to-EBITDA at 2.1x" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg). "We recently entered into a 2-year delayed draw term loan of up to $500 million" and "we now expect to deploy at least $800 million towards share repurchases or acquisitions in 2026, up from our previous expectation of approximately $600 million."

Q&A

  • John Lovallo, UBS: Asked about what could drive potential tariff favorability and how to quantify it; VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg: "we look at it from a composite perspective... we expect a favorable impact... but it's difficult to quantify" and "any favorability will likely be offset by elevated commodity costs."
  • Stephen Kim, Evercore ISI: Asked for sizing and cadence of tariff/commodity offsets; President, CEO & Director Nudi: "We're not going to quantify" and said it is "largely a back-half of 2026 phenomenon" for plumbing commodities, while oil/resins are "more near term."
  • Richard Reid, Wells Fargo: Asked if plumbing volume had one-time or pull-forward effects and for pricing by channel; President, CEO & Director Nudi: "the short answer is no" on one-times, and on channel pricing "We typically don't get into that level of detail."
  • Matthew Bouley, Barclays: Pressed on plumbing growth deceleration implied by full-year guide and on Europe demand/energy; President, CEO & Director Nudi: "it's the uncertainty that we see in the world around us that... caused us to keep our guidance" and "We like how Europe is holding up... China... remains a challenging market."
  • Ketan Mamtora, BMO: Asked where results land within the EPS range if demand stays as-is and about buybacks vs. M&A; VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg: "you can comfortably assume that we'll end in the mid part of the range" and said increased capacity is "opportunistic" with "an increase in share repurchases from $600 million to $800 million plus absent any M&A."
  • Michael Dahl, RBC: Questioned levers to offset broad input/tariff headwinds and tariff refund strategy; VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg: "the levers remain the same" ("footprint," "restructuring," and "pricing"), while President, CEO & Director Nudi said, "we are not banking on refunds."

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts were slightly negative to pressing on uncertainty and offsets, focusing on quantification and cadence, including "how much" (Evercore) and how to "wrap your arms around" tariff benefit (UBS).
  • Management tone was slightly positive but guarded, repeatedly emphasizing uncertainty and limited quantification, including "We're not going to quantify" (President, CEO & Director Jonathon Nudi) and "it's difficult to quantify" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Richard Westenberg).
  • Versus last quarter, management language shifted from expecting "margin contraction in the first half" (Q4) to "relatively flat in the first half" (Q1), while analysts continued to concentrate on tariffs, commodities, and demand durability.

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Guidance range for EPS stayed the same at "$4.10 to $4.30" (both quarters), while the sales outlook language moved to "now be up low single digits" (Q1) from "flat to up low single digits" (Q4).
  • Capital deployment expectation increased to "at least $800 million" in 2026 (Q1) from "approximately $600 million" (Q4), supported by "a 2-year delayed draw term loan of up to $500 million" (Q1).
  • Management updated the margin cadence narrative to "relatively flat" in the first half (Q1) versus "margin contraction" in the first half (Q4), while keeping the back-half expansion framework tied to lapping tariff impacts.

Risks and concerns

  • "High degree of uncertainty in the macroeconomic and geopolitical environment" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Richard Westenberg) remained central, with management highlighting that "China... remains a challenging market" (President, CEO & Director Jonathon Nudi).
  • Commodity risk was framed as potentially offsetting tariff relief: "any tailwind from these tariff changes will be more than offset by anticipated increases in commodity and related input costs" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg).
  • Working capital was described as elevated due to tariff timing: "working capital balances in the first half of the year remained elevated" (VP, CFO & Treasurer Westenberg), with an expectation it ends the year "approximately 16.5%" of sales.

Final takeaway

Masco’s management highlighted Q1 execution and reiterated full-year EPS guidance of $4.10 to $4.30 amid heightened macro, geopolitical, tariff, and commodity uncertainty. Leadership changes and a streamlined reporting structure were positioned as enabling faster decision-making, while restructuring actions (targeting approximately $50 million of 2026 charges) and ongoing cost initiatives were presented as key margin supports. Capital allocation became more aggressive with a new delayed-draw facility of up to $500 million and a higher 2026 deployment expectation of at least $800 million toward repurchases or acquisitions, while management emphasized it is not relying on tariff refunds and expects commodity inflation to remain a key swing factor later in the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: OpenAI introduces Privacy Filter model
data.description:
• OpenAI (OPENAI) took the wraps off its Privacy Filter model on Wednesday, which the ChatGPT maker says is aimed at “detecting and redacting personally identifiable information in text.”
  • “Privacy Filter is a small model with frontier personal data detection capability,” OpenAI said in a blog post. “It is designed for high-throughput privacy workflows, and is able to perform context-aware detection of PII in unstructured text. It can run locally, which means that PII can be masked or redacted without leaving your machine. It processes long inputs efficiently, making redaction decisions in a quick, single pass.”
  • The model goes across eight categories: names of people, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, URLs, dates, account numbers and a category called secret.
  • The released model has 1.5B total parameters with 50M active parameters and is available today on Hugging Face⁠ and Github.

data.title: Soaring costs hampering efforts by U.S. coal producers to boost exports
data.description:
U.S. coal producers see the Middle East war as an opportunity to boost sales and push into international markets, as the blockage in the Strait of Hormuz has helped demand for coal, but rising fuel costs are complicating their efforts, the Financial Times reported Wednesday.

"It's frustrating, Core Natural Resources (CNR) CEO Jimmy Brock told FT. "Diesel plays a tremendous part in our costs, and because of the crisis, the price just keeps rising."

Brock said he expects Core Natural's (CNR) exports to rise 10% this year and that it is takingorders from "surprise markets" such as Indonesia and Vietnam, in addition to typical coal importers such as India and China.

But U.S. producers are at a geographical disadvantage compared to producers in Australia that are closer to Asian markets, making them more vulnerable to rising transportation costs.

Coal producers are heavily reliant on diesel-powered machinery, as well as trucks, trains, and barges used to move their coal from mines to utility customers and ports for export, and the price of diesel for trucks has surged 48% since the Iran war began, according to data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Meanwhile, the cost of shipping from North America to China is up by as much as 55%, Bank of America has said.

U.S. producers with overseas operations have managed to benefit from the crisis, includingPeabody Energy (BTU), which ships coal to Asia from its Australian mines.

Peabody (BTU) CEO Jim Grech told FT the company is enjoying "stronger demand andpricing" for its exports, "given the scarcity and soaring prices" of liquefied natural gas in Asia.

Other potentially relevant stocks include Warrior Met Coal (HCC), Ramaco Resources (METC), Alpha Metallurgical Resources (AMR), Alliance Resource Partners (ARLP), Whitehaven Coal (WHITF), Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY).

data.title: Nasdaq touches new high as Trump extends ceasefire deadline with Iran
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) reaching a new intraday high, as U.S. President Donald Trump announced an extension to the ceasefire deadline with Iran while citing deep divisions within Tehran’s leadership.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.8%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.3% and hitting new intraday highs. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.7%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point lower at 4.29%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.81%.

Trump said on Tuesday that the U.S. would extend its ceasefire with Iran, citing deep divisions within Tehran's leadership. He also said this ceasefire would remain in place until a coordinated plan for an end to the war, which would include Israel, was brought forward. However, the ongoing blockade is expected to continue.

In addition, Trump reportedly said negotiation could take place once again as soon as Friday, according to The New York Post.

On the other hand, Iran claimed to have seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz that crossed “without authorization,” according to state news agency Tasnim.

Previously, Vice President JD Vance had called off a planned trip to Pakistan until further notice, casting doubt on last-minute ceasefire negotiations between the U.S. and Iran, according to a media report.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) surged to $92, while Brent (CO1:COM) also rose to $101 per barrel.

In other news, Bitcoin (BTC-USD)—currently at around $79,300—touched a 10-week high, as market sentiment improved.

On the U.S. economy, the Atlanta Fed Business Inflation Expectations rose higher than expected in March.
